value: 일본 국외 거주 외국인 여권 소지자. 'Temporary Visitor' 체류자격으로 입국한 자만 구매 가능. 일본 국적자 또는 일본 거주 외국인은 구매 불가.

value.비고: 2023년 10월 인상 후 유지. 2025-2026 추가 인상 없음.

value: 한국 국적자는 90일 이내 관광 목적 무비자 입국 가능. 여권 잔여 유효기간 제한 없음 (체류 기간 이상 권장). Visit Japan Web 사전 등록 권장.
Suica/PASMO 등 IC카드로 전철, 버스, 편의점 결제 가능. 물리 카드 재고 부족은 2025.3월 공식 해소. Welcome Suica(관광객용, 28일 유효) 주요 역에서 판매 재개. 모바일 Suica(Apple/Google) 및 Welcome Suica Mobile(iPhone, 180일 유효) 이용 가능.
지정석(Reserved)과 자유석(Non-reserved) 구분. JR Pass 소지 시 지정석 무료 예약 가능 (Nozomi/Mizuho 제외). 자유석은 선착순 탑승.
면세 범위: 주류 3병(760ml 이하), 담배 200개비(거주자)/400개비(비거주자), 향수 2oz, 기타 품목 20만엔 이하. 초과 시 신고 및 관세 부과.

value.비고: 구급차 무료, 치료비는 별도. 119/110은 일본어 우선이나 통역 연결 가능.

value: Visit Japan Web은 웹 기반 서비스(앱 불필요). 여권 정보, 입국심사(하선카드), 세관신고, 면세쇼핑 3개 항목 사전 등록. 등록 후 QR코드 발급 → 공항 도착 시 제시. 2026년 현재 필수는 아니나 강력 권장 (심사 시간 대폭 단축). 스크린샷 불가, 라이브 접속 필요.

신제도_2026_11월.방식: 환급형 (구매 시 세금 포함 → 출국 시 환급)

value: Tourists in Japan are required to have private medical insurance starting in 2025 to cover medical costs during their stay, including emergencies.
Foreigners can use Visit Japan Web for immigration procedures (immigration clearance, customs declaration) and tax-free purchases during their stay in Japan.
Both non-Japanese and returning Japanese citizens are eligible to import personal effects into Japan under the customs allowance guidelines.
Japan grants visa-free entry to citizens of 74 countries and regions for short-term tourism as of 2025.
Average accommodation cost included in a $110 daily travel budget, with accommodation price halved
A full meal in tourist-populated areas in Japan can be found for approximately 10 CAD, including tax, covering fast food and teishoku set meals.
The Japan Rail Pass is valid on almost all trains operated on the nationwide JR network, including shinkansen, limited express, rapid, and local trains, but generally not valid on non-JR trains.

Admission prices at major shrines and temples in Japan range from free entry to ¥1,000 and more, e.g., Tōji temple ¥500, Kōsanji temple ¥1,000, Tenryūji temple ¥500, Tōdaiji temple ¥600, Kōfukuji temple ¥700, Kasuga Taisha Shrine free with ¥500 for special admission
As of 2026, Suica can be used nationwide in Japan due to the IC card interoperability system, allowing a Suica purchased in Tokyo to be used on subways in Osaka and buses in Fukuoka.
Hearton Hotel Kyoto is located in Kyoto
Tipping is not a part of Japanese culture due to the concept of Omotenashi, which embodies hospitality as a cultural value rather than a service for tips.
IC cards such as Suica, PASMO, and ICOCA should not be left unused for a decade; it is advised not to let a decade lapse before using your IC card again
Icoca card costs ¥2000 including a ¥500 deposit
House and apartment rentals are available all over Japan

The Welcome Suica is currently the only card-type IC card available for travelers to purchase in Japan, and it can be bought at designated locations and charged at black multifunction ticket machines.
Many hotels in Japan, especially ryokans, require guests to remove their shoes at the entrance or in their room.
Japan is an island country in East Asia, located in the Pacific Ocean
Most museums, temples, castles and gardens charge an admission of at least a few hundred yen
Many businesses in Japan are cash-only, so travelers should be prepared to carry cash.
Typical dinner time in Japan generally falls between 6:00 p.m. and 8:00 p.m., though it can vary depending on individual household routines and work or school schedules.

Kaiseki is Japan’s most refined dining, almost synonymous with Western haute cuisine, and requires the highest level of etiquette and respect for the food, typically prepared by experienced professional chefs.
A reasonable Japanese-style dinner in Tokyo or elsewhere costs around $20 (¥2000), typically including a set menu with rice, soup, salad, and a simple main dish.
Just a 6-minute walk from Shinjuku Station
You can pre-order Pocket WiFi online before your trip to Japan, and it can be rented at airports or delivered to your hotel for pickup.
Pocket Wi-Fi rental devices can be reserved before arrival in Japan and picked up at the airport for instant access to reliable connectivity.

Physical SUICA and PASMO IC travel cards must be purchased in Japan, commonly available at airports or train stations upon arrival, and can be used to make payments at many convenience stores, shops, vending machines, and for public transportation.
Wi-FiRental.com charges ¥6,600/month with ¥1,100 free with discount code 'cheapo' at checkout

Tourists in Japan can choose from several mobile internet options including pocket WiFi, public WiFi, roaming, SIM cards, and eSIMs, with major carriers like AU/KDDI and Softbank offering nationwide coverage and various plan options.
The most commonly accepted contactless payment smartphone apps or QR code payment methods in Japan are PayPay, LinePay, Rpay (Rakuten Pay), and MerPay.
Average amount spent in Japan: $110 a day
Japan is an island country in East Asia, located in the Pacific Ocean, divided into 9 regions: Hokkaido, Tohoku, Kanto, Chubu, Kansai (Kinki), Chugoku, Shikoku, Kyushu, and Okinawa.
Tipping is not common in Japan for services such as those provided in bars, cafes, restaurants, taxis, and hotels.
The Icoca card costs ¥2000, which includes a ¥500 deposit and ¥1500 of credit
Beat the crowds by visiting attractions early, as many popular attractions in Japan become unbearably crowded by midday.
Most major konbini chains support contactless payment services like Apple Pay and Google Pay, and are equipped with ATMs that accept foreign-issued credit cards for withdrawing Japanese yen.
Credit cards such as Visa, JCB, and Mastercard are widely accepted in Japan, but travelers should be prepared to pay with cash as a backup due to possible payment errors.

The most commonly accepted contactless payment smartphone apps or QR code payment methods in Japan are PayPay, LinePay, Rpay (Rakuten Pay), and MerPay. Most stores also support credit cards such as Visa, JCB, or Mastercard.

Balance-3 payment acceptance is not explicitly mentioned in the sources; however, major credit card brands such as Visa, Mastercard, and JCB are widely accepted in Japan, including at convenience stores like 7-Eleven, and mobile payment apps such as PayPay, LinePay, Rakuten Pay, and MerPay are commonly used.

Kaiseki is Japan’s most refined dining, almost synonymous with Western haute cuisine, requiring the highest level of etiquette and respect for the food, typically prepared by experienced professional chefs.
Japan uses many international payment methods such as Apple Pay and Google Pay, and domestic payment methods like PayPay are popular for QR code payments.
Japan is still a predominantly cash-based country, with cash payments making up 82% of all payments in 2018.
The Japan Rail Pass can be purchased for 7 days, 14 days, or 21 days
Many convenience stores such as 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, and Lawson, as well as some chain restaurants and discount stores, operate 24 hours a day, even on national holidays.
The Suica validity period is 180 days from the issuance date for Welcome Suica Mobile cards.
All JR stations display station names in both Japanese and romanized English on their platforms, and some stations use station numbering.

Small local restaurants in Tokyo can feel intimidating if there is no English menu or the staff doesn’t speak much English, so being adventurous and stepping outside usual tourist zones helps to eat like a true local.

You need to purchase a JR Pass Exchange Order from an authorized sales agent in the US prior to your arrival in Japan. Present your Exchange Order and obtain your JR Pass after you arrive in Japan at one of the Japan Rail Pass exchange offices.
Suica cards can be purchased by anyone at major JR stations in Eastern Japan, including Tokyo area stations such as Tokyo, Hamamatsucho, Shimbashi, Shinjuku, and Shibuya.
Physical Suica cards must be purchased in Japan and cannot be bought online before arrival; they are commonly available at airports (e.g., Welcome Suica) or train stations upon arrival.
Fastest travel time between Tokyo Station and Narita Airport: 65 minutes
The Japan Rail Pass is the best travel companion for riding Shinkansen bullet trains and exploring coastal and rural routes in Japan.
The Tokyo to Kyoto Shinkansen can be covered in just over two hours.

IC cards like Suica, Pasmo, and ICOCA are rechargeable smart cards that can be used on trains, subways, buses, and even at vending machines or convenience stores. They work across most regions in Japan, including travel between Tokyo and Kyoto.
A central hotel location is essential for convenience and accessibility, enhancing the travel experience in Japan.
Tokyo to Kyoto Shinkansen can be covered in just over two hours
Many convenience stores such as 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, and Lawson, as well as some chain restaurants and discount stores, operate 24 hours a day, even on national holidays.

Japanese convenience stores offer a huge variety of fresh, high-quality food including onigiri, sushi, sandwiches, salads, bento boxes, noodle dishes, single-serving fruit, baked goods, beverages, candy, and snacks at budget-friendly prices.

To use SoftBank's Prepaid SIM for Travel, users must set up and apply before using the 4G prepaid SIM by accessing the special Prepaid SIM for Travel website, tapping 'Plan purchase', entering the URL or scanning the QR code, confirming notes before purchasing, checking 'Agree', and tapping 'Next'.
Booking a connectivity solution with NINJA WiFi before arrival allows travelers to move directly from customs to transportation without delays and ensures dependable internet access throughout Japan.
Daibutsu-den Hall opening hours are from 7:30 to 17:30
For opening hours of temples and facilities for tourists in Kyoto, please refer to the 'Morning Sightseeing/Night Sightseeing' website, which provides tips to enjoy Kyoto in the morning and at night.
On January 1, most shops and restaurants in Tokyo open from 11am to 7pm; on January 2 and 3, shops open 11am-7pm and restaurants 11am-9pm; some shops open 10am-8pm with restaurants open 11am-11pm
Almost all restaurants and shops are open between 10am-10pm on January 1st

For specific Japan eSIMs like Journey Japan eSIM, you can purchase your data plan, install the eSIM on your device when you arrive in Japan, and connect instantly. Plans offer 30-day trip coverage with prices starting as low as $0.98/GB.
Mobile phone and Wi-Fi rental counters as well as SIM-card sales counters can only be found in areas before departure passport control at Narita Airport.
